description:
## Optimization-quality signal

Now that any-key is the soundness floor, specialization is a pure
optimization. We want a knob to tell us "this call fell through to the
generic dispatch when a narrower spec exists or could have existed."

## Change

In `spec_check.rs` (or as a pass invoked at end-of-compile when
`--Wspec-quality` is set):
- Walk every direct-call site in every spec body.
- For each, recompute the codegen's Descr query (`block_env`-narrowed args).
- Run `resolve`; if the chosen spec is the any-key AND there exists a
  narrower registered spec keyed strictly above `[any; arity]`, emit:
  ```
  warning[spec-quality/any-key-fallback]: call to `f/2` dispatched to any-key spec
    --> file:line
     | the typer registered narrower specs but none covers the inferred call-site Descrs
     = note: dispatch is correct but may be slower; consider whether the typer's
       narrowing is incomplete at this call site
  ```

Gated behind `--Wspec-quality` (off by default; CI can enable for regression
catching).

## Tests

- A fixture with a deliberately-pessimized typing (e.g. function called with
  `any` because it's stored in a heterogeneous list) emits the warning.
- The existing fixture matrix passes with the flag off.
- A new `spec_quality_clean` test asserts the matrix passes with the flag on
  for fixtures we expect to be fully specialized (`vr*`).

## Blocked by fz-aiz.5. P3 — nice-to-have, not required for soundness.
title: fz-aiz.6: --Wspec-quality diagnostic when call dispatches to any-key
